event.summary: Prepared a source-first, planning-last push bundle without executing Git push.

acceptance.proof: The observation binds an exact official public package, native library digest/version, documented public API calls, independently authored probe digest, bounded command, sanitized environment, and external raw-artifact hashes without source inspection.

At least two deterministic synthetic endpoint cases return within fixed time and byte limits on loopback, and sanitized facts retain exact response length, line-delimiter and field-boundary positions, correlation spelling/location, and document-region hash relationships while removing runtime/host/address/port character data.

Raw datagrams, XML, diagnostics, environments, caches, and machine paths remain external; the committed fixture contains only synthetic caller inputs, sanitized facts, hashes, provenance, limitations, and explicit nonclaims.
No production Rust, Cargo, feature lock, project spec, dependency, socket, runtime, device, activation, endpoint authority, interoperability claim, or Manifold authority changes.

document.commit_policy: Commit observation provenance and sanitized facts separately from any later response-byte implementation or typed binding.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the observation-only evidence boundary and any exact observed response framing limitations.
Describe the observation and distinguish it from response implementation and interoperability.
Review public/private and Rusty LSL ownership routing; retain unchanged if existing guidance is sufficient.
Review evidence-producer, artifact-role, validation, and authority separation; retain unchanged if current guidance is sufficient.

non_scope: Rust response encoding, parsing, semantic admission, query correlation, endpoint interpretation, sockets or discovery runtime in the production crate.
Multicast or broadcast probing, non-loopback traffic, interface selection, retries, clocks, currentness, interoperability claims, devices, dependencies, features, unsafe, FFI, or runtime activation.
Implementation-source inspection or use of liblsl, pylsl, rLSL, wrapper, generated, vendored, test, or application source as an implementation input.
Publication of raw datagrams, raw XML, native diagnostics, local paths, host/interface values, runtime identifiers, credentials, device identities, or private planning history.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, session, topology, revision, replay, authorization, audit, transport, product, command, or provider authority.
document.objective: Observe the smallest protocol-110 short-info response envelope from one exact official public liblsl endpoint through documented StreamInfo and StreamOutlet APIs plus a loopback-only independently authored UDP probe, publishing only sanitized byte-shape facts sufficient to decide a later source-only response contract.
outputs: One provenance-locked public-safe sanitized observation fixture for the exact short-info response envelope.
A focused validator that fails closed on provenance, raw/private leakage, framing facts, case count, bounds, and nonclaims.
An evidence decision stating whether a later response envelope and LSLC-002A/002B binding are justified, with unresolved semantics explicitly deferred.
